category_title: Young children with persistent challenging behaviors across multiple routines
minimal_context: Kayden is at home and is being prompted to start getting ready for bed.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Kayden, it is time to start getting ready for bed.

STUDENT: (Starts crying)

TUTOR: Let's go get your pajamas.

STUDENT: (Screams)

TUTOR: We have to get ready for bed now.

STUDENT: (Screams and begins hitting)

options.A: Grant full agency during the routine by telling Kayden, 'I don't care, just pick out any pajamas that you would probably like.'
options.B: Joke a lot and laugh at oneself while attempting to prompt Kayden to get ready for bed during the routine.
options.C: Offer to read a favorite book or sing a favorite song in bed after Kayden puts on his pajamas, just before deciding to prompt him.
options.D: Have a puppet try to 'eat' Kayden's fingers, extending this action into holding and jiggling his arms during the bedtime routine.
